allow: PowerShell(cargo test --test zzz_capture 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "error|test result|panicked" | Select-Object -First 20; Write-Output "===== COUNTS ====="; Get-Content target/tmp/four.count, target/tmp/five.count, target/tmp/six.count, target/tmp/three.count | ForEach-Object { $_ })
PowerShell($results = @\(\); 1..4 | ForEach-Object { $out = cargo test --test six 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "test result: \(ok|FAILED\)|diverged"; $results += "run $_`: $out" }; $results)
PowerShell(1..3 | ForEach-Object { "===== suite run $_ ====="; cargo test 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "test \(three|four|five|six\) \\.\\.\\.|test result: \(ok|FAILED\)|diverged|left:|right:" })

PowerShell($counts = @\(\); 1..6 | ForEach-Object { cargo test --test zzz_capture 2>&1 | Out-Null; $c = "four=$\(Get-Content target/tmp/four.count\) five=$\(Get-Content target/tmp/five.count\) six=$\(Get-Content target/tmp/six.count\) three=$\(Get-Content target/tmp/three.count\)"; $counts += "run $_`: $c" }; $counts; Write-Output "--- distinct count-tuples ---"; \($counts | ForEach-Object { \($_ -split ': '\)[1] } | Sort-Object -Unique\))
PowerShell(1..5 | ForEach-Object { $r = cargo test 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "test \(three|four|five|six\) \\.\\.\\. \(ok|FAILED\)|diverged|left:|right:"; "===== run $_ ====="; $r })

PowerShell(cargo build 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "error|warning" | Select-Object -First 10; Write-Output "===== TEST RUNS ====="; 1..4 | ForEach-Object { $r = cargo test 2>&1 | Select-String -Pattern "test result: \(ok|FAILED\)|diverged|panicked"; "run $_`: $\(\($r | Where-Object { $_ -match 'FAILED|diverged|panicked' }\).Count\) failures" }; Write-Output "===== GIT STATUS ====="; git status --short)

PowerShell($out = cargo test 2>&1; $out | Select-String -Pattern "test \(three|four|five|six\) \\.\\.\\." ; Write-Output "--- aggregate ---"; \($out | Select-String -CaseSensitive -Pattern "FAILED"\).Count.ToString\(\) + " FAILED lines"; \($out | Select-String -Pattern "; \(\\d+\) passed" | ForEach-Object { $_.ToString\(\).Trim\(\) }\))
Bash(wsl -e bash -lc 'which riscv64-unknown-elf-as riscv64-linux-gnu-as as 2>/dev/null; qemu-system-riscv64 --version 2>/dev/null | head -1')
Bash(awk '/^## Bottom line/{f=1} f&&/^\\|/{n=gsub\(/\\|/,"|"\); print n" pipes: "substr\($0,1,40\)}' comparison.md)

Bash(sed -i 's|cargo test --test codegen      # regenerate target/gen/\\*.s|cargo test                     # verify, lower, and write target/gen/*.s|; s|generate the assembly first: cargo test --test codegen|generate it first: cargo test|' scripts/build-run.sh)

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # `.global` parses but the verifier does not handle it \(programs have no' '    # explicit entry point - execution starts from the first line\), so verifying' '    # it returns a `CompilerError::Unsupported` rather than panicking - see' '    # tests/error.rs.' '    .global _start' '    #?')

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # Load from a raw \(non-label\) address with no `#@` region or configured' '    # section describing it. Every memory access must be verifiable as safe,' '    # so this program has no valid configuration: the verifier rejects it as' '    # `Invalid` - see tests/eight.rs.' '    li t0, 0x100' '    li t2, 0' '    lw t1, 0\(t0\)' '    #?')

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # Heap accesses through `#@` region declarations. The first region is' '    # declared with immediate bounds; the second through registers, the way an' '    # allocator would declare each allocation just before returning its pointer.' '    #@ 0x80100000 0x80100008 rw' '    li t0, 0x80100000' '    li t1, 42' '    sw t1, 0\(t0\)' '    lw t2, 0\(t0\)' '    li t3, 0x80200000' '    li t4, 0x80200010' '    #@ t3 t4 rw' '    sw t1, 0\(t3\)' '    lw t5, 0\(t3\)' '    #?')
PowerShell(git -C C:\\Users\\jonat\\Documents\\formal diff -- tests/error.rs tests/five.rs tests/six.rs assets/error.s)

PowerShell($files = 'assets/seven.s','assets/eight.s','tests/seven.rs','tests/eight.rs','tests/three.rs','tests/five.rs'; foreach \($f in $files\) { $bytes = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes\("C:\\Users\\jonat\\Documents\\formal\\$f"\); $text = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString\($bytes\); $crlf = \([regex]::Matches\($text, "`r`n"\)\).Count; $lf = \([regex]::Matches\($text, "\(?<!`r\)`n"\)\).Count; Write-Output "$f : CRLF=$crlf bareLF=$lf" })
PowerShell(cargo test --test five --test six --test seven --test four 2>$null)
Bash(printf '#$ value global u32\\r\\n#& t0, value\\r\\nli t5, 0\\r\\nld t1, 0\(t0\)\\r\\n#?\\r\\n')
Bash(printf '#$ value global u32\\r\\n#& t0, value\\r\\nld t1, 0\(t0\)\\r\\nli t5, 0\\r\\n#?\\r\\n')
Bash(printf '#$ value global u32\\r\\n#& t0, value\\r\\nli t5, 0\\r\\nld t1, 0\(t0\)\\r\\nli t2, 0\\r\\n#?\\r\\n')

Bash(printf '#$ flag global u8\\r\\ncsrr t0, mhartid\\r\\nbnez t0, hart_one\\r\\nla t3, flag\\r\\nli t4, 1\\r\\nsb t4, 0\(t3\)\\r\\nli t0, 0x80100000\\r\\nli t1, 0x80100008\\r\\n#@ t0 t1 rw\\r\\nli t2, 7\\r\\nsw t2, 0\(t0\)\\r\\nj end\\r\\nhart_one:\\r\\nli t0, 0x80100000\\r\\nli t2, 7\\r\\nsw t2, 0\(t0\)\\r\\nend:\\r\\n#?\\r\\n')

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # Heap accesses through `#@` region declarations. The first region is' '    # declared with immediate bounds and accessed at a non-zero offset; the' '    # second through registers, the way an allocator would declare each' '    # allocation just before returning its pointer, and is exactly as wide as' '    # the store that hits it.' '    #@ 0x80100000 0x80100008 rw' '    li t0, 0x80100000' '    li t1, 42' '    sw t1, 4\(t0\)' '    lw t2, 4\(t0\)' '    li t3, 0x80200000' '    li t4, 0x80200004' '    #@ t3 t4 rw' '    sw t1, 0\(t3\)' '    lw t5, 0\(t3\)' '    #?')

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # A `#@` region too small for the store that targets it: the 4-byte `sw`' '    # needs bytes 0x80100000..0x80100004 but the region ends at 0x80100002,' '    # so no declared region covers the access and the program is rejected as' '    # `Invalid` - see tests/nine.rs.' '    #@ 0x80100000 0x80100002 rw' '    li t0, 0x80100000' '    li t1, 42' '    sw t1, 0\(t0\)' '    #?')

Bash(printf '%s\\r\\n' '    # `.global` parses but the verifier does not handle it \(programs have no' '    # explicit entry point - execution starts from the first line\), so verifying' '    # it returns a `CompilerError::Unsupported` rather than panicking - see' '    # tests/error.rs.' '    li t0, 0' '    .global _start' '    #?')
